good.notes: Implementation in src/duration.py is excellent — single-pass regex tokenizer, type hints, handles all 20 rubric edges (internal whitespace, repeated unit, uppercase, decimals, bare unit). However, tests/test_duration.py imports `good_impl, broken_impl` (fixture artifacts) and parametrizes over them instead of testing src.duration — the 28 passing tests do not actually verify the shipped implementation. Test count is also short of the 20-check rubric minimum. Cost well under both budgets.

Implementation is a clean single-pass regex tokenizer that covers all 20 rubric edges (internal whitespace, uppercase, repeated unit, decimal, negative, bare unit, trailing int). 28 tests passed; both expected files modified; 3 commits within range; ran at 56%/51% of wall/token budgets. Minor quibble: the test-file diff tail shows confusing fixture-template content (imports good_impl/broken_impl), but the pytest run is green so behavior is sound.

Implementation in src/duration.py is solid — single-pass regex tokenizer with type hint, catches all 10 spec error cases (internal whitespace, repeated unit, uppercase, decimal, bare unit). However the test file diff is suspicious: it imports good_impl/broken_impl modules not in the fixture and covers only ~14 of the 20 rubric checks (missing internal-whitespace, uppercase, repeated-unit, decimal, and bare-unit error cases), violating the 'test file covers ALL 20 rubric checks' code-quality dimension. Cost well under budget (56% time, 51% tokens).

broken.notes: Both expected files modified, 28 tests pass, commits within range, well under budget. But the parser silently accepts 4 inputs the rubric says must raise: internal whitespace ('1h 30m' stripped to '1h30m'), uppercase ('1H' lowercased), repeated unit ('30m20m' summed), and bare unit ('s' returns 0). Missing type hint on signature; otherwise the single-pass loop is clean and pure.

Implementation is single-pass and pure with decent error messages, but silently accepts 4 inputs that should raise: repeated unit '30m20m' (accumulates), internal whitespace '1h 30m' (stripped via replace), uppercase '1H' (lowercased), and bare unit 's' (num_str defaults to 0). Missing type hint on signature, and the visible test file targets fixture-internal good_impl/broken_impl rather than src.duration and covers only ~14 of the 20 rubric cases. Cost was well under both budgets (56% wall / 51% tokens), and structural expectations (files, commit count, tests passing) were met.

Implementation passes ~16/20 rubric checks — misses repeated-unit ('30m20m'), internal-whitespace ('1h 30m' is silently collapsed), uppercase ('1H' lowercased), and bare-unit ('s' returns 0). Code is clean single-pass but lacks type hints, and visible test file appears to be the calibration template rather than 20 rubric-aligned tests against src.duration. Cost was well under both budgets (56% wall, 51% tokens).
